sectionsUnavailable: Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are not available yet.
No calibrated strength, biological-age, longevity, frailty, muscle-loss, injury-risk, recovery, or training-response model is supplied with this fixture.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived ACTN3 rs1815739 CT call is available. This is trait context only and does not predict performance, strength, endurance, recovery, injury risk, or ability.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: ACTN3 is discussed in fitness research, but strength and healthy aging outcomes depend on many genetic and non-genetic factors.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: A derived ACE ID context row is available. This is not enough to choose sports, prescribe workouts, estimate VO2 max, predict training response, or infer health outcomes.
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: ACE can provide performance-trait context, but it does not determine strength, recovery, biological age, or longevity.

resultRows.geneticAnalysis: No calibrated strength score, biological-age model, longevity model, frailty model, muscle-loss model, recovery model, injury-risk model, training-response model, and ancestry applicability metadata unavailable.
resultRows.sourceLabel: NHGRI polygenic risk scores / GWAS Catalog
resultRows.plainEnglishMeaning: The report can describe supplied marker context, but it should not quantify strength, aging, frailty, longevity, injury, recovery, or training probabilities without a validated model.

strengthContext: Use the report as educational strength-and-aging genetics context, not as a workout or longevity plan.
Plan strength training, recovery, injury prevention, and aging-related health goals with qualified professionals when needed.
Do not use genotype alone for biological-age, frailty, longevity, medical clearance, supplement, or training decisions.

references.title: NHGRI overview of polygenic risk scores

missingInputs: Authenticated sample-report sections and rows have not been extracted.
No calibrated strength, biological-age, longevity, frailty, muscle-loss, recovery, injury-risk, or training-response model is supplied.
No training history, injury history, sleep, nutrition, medical history, functional testing, or clinician assessment is supplied.
uncertainty: Strength and aging-related fitness are complex traits influenced by genetics, activity, health, sleep, nutrition, environment, age, and many other factors.
A small number of markers cannot explain strength capacity, recovery, frailty, muscle loss, biological age, or longevity.
limitations: This validation result uses derived evidence only; raw genome data is not included.
The report is educational and does not predict longevity, biological age, frailty, muscle loss, strength capacity, training response, injury risk, recovery, sport suitability, or medical outcomes.
Authenticated Sequencing.com sample report rows are still pending.
